zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Mainwaring III , Knight於1286年出生在英國英格蘭爾米德列維其時，他的父親Roger de Mainwaring23歲，母親Christiana de Birtles21歲。他於1315年與Lady Mary Davenport在英國爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1339年在英國英格蘭爾奧維 奧福享年53歲，埋葬在英國爾馬頓。

nameMeanings.name: Mainwaring
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mesnil-Varin in Saint-Paër (Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie). The placename means ‘domain of Warin’ (see   Waring  ). Compare   Manwaring   and   Mandry  .
Irish: adopted for Ó Manaráin, see Mannering 2.

variants.name: Manwaring
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mainwaring  .

variants.name: Waring
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Warren , from the Norman personal name Warin, derived from ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France and among the Normans (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Mainwaring III , Knight was born in 1286, in Middlewich, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Roger de Mainwaring, was 23 and his mother, Christiana de Birtles, was 21. He married Lady Mary Davenport in 1315, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1339, in Over Peover, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53, and was buried in Marton, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Mainwaring III , Knight est né en 1286, à Middlewich, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger de Mainwaring, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Christiana de Birtles, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Lady Mary Davenport en 1315, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1339, à Over Peover, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Marton, Cheshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Mainwaring III , Knightが1286年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアミッドルイチで生まれた時，父Roger de Mainwaringは23歳，母Christiana de Birtlesは21歳であった。1315年にイギリスチェシュアイアでLady Mary Davenportと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1339年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアオバー ペオバーで53歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアマルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Mainwaring III , Knight(이)가 1286년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 미들루이치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger de Mainwaring(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Christiana de Birtles(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1315년 영국 체실르에서 Lady Mary Davenport(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1339년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 버 프오버에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 마턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Mainwaring III , Knight родился в 1286 г. в местности миддльвич, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger de Mainwaring было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Christiana de Birtles, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Mary Davenport в 1315 г. в местности чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1339 г. в местности овир пеовир, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности мартон, чешир, Англия.
